summary: ISS-965 investigated. Fetched current dome site (V51.1, 2026-04-14) and confirmed: (a) r = 0.992 IS explicitly labeled on the dome home page Exhibit A as 'Raw L1A residuals' — a temporal correlation, not a slope fit; (b) r = 0.96 does NOT appear on the current dome home, predictions, or context pages — PRED-KAPPA-001 in V51.1 is about the ECLIPSE correction factor, not the 2003 storm. The 'mutually inconsistent for the same dataset' argument is therefore not defensible against a statistically literate defender. EXP-160 rewrites the argument to: accept the dome's own label on r = 0.992, show that this labeled statistic (raw L1A temporal correlation during a G5 storm) is exactly what shared-electromagnetic-forcing physics predicts to run high, and ground the weakness on the uncontestable fact that NO repository code computes either r-value or the 10.9/6.5 ratio. Patches finding, detail_evidence, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict. Also collapses a duplicated 'Note on dome-site numbering' paragraph. Decider: please also flip WIN-058b.code_analysis.reviewed to true (c1+c2 both validated the tags) — flagged in EXP-160.decider_hints.flip_reviewed_flag.
